5.title: Principles relating to processing of personal data
5.summary: Lawfulness, fairness, transparency, purpose limitation, data minimisation, accuracy, storage limitation, integrity, confidentiality, and accountability.

6.title: Lawfulness of processing
6.summary: Processing is lawful only to the extent that at least one of six legal bases applies: consent, contract, legal obligation, vital interests, public task, or legitimate interests.

7.title: Conditions for consent
7.summary: Where processing is based on consent, the controller must demonstrate that the data subject has consented, and withdrawal must be as easy as giving consent.

9.title: Processing of special categories of personal data
9.summary: Processing of biometric, genetic, health, political, religious, or other special category data is prohibited unless explicit consent or specific legal exceptions apply.

12.title: Transparent information, communication and modalities for exercise of data subject rights
12.summary: Controllers must provide information in a concise, transparent, intelligible, and easily accessible form, using clear and plain language.

13.title: Information to be provided where personal data are collected from the data subject
13.summary: Mandatory disclosures including controller identity, purpose, legal basis, legitimate interests, recipients, retention period, and data subject rights.

14.title: Information to be provided where personal data have not been obtained from the data subject
14.summary: Similar to Art. 13 but applies when data is obtained from third parties, with additional notification timelines and source disclosure requirements.

15.title: Right of access by the data subject
15.summary: Data subjects have the right to obtain confirmation of processing, access to their data, and information about processing purposes, categories, recipients, and storage duration.

17.title: Right to erasure ('right to be forgotten')
17.summary: Data subjects have the right to obtain erasure of personal data without undue delay where specific grounds apply, including withdrawal of consent or unlawful processing.

20.title: Right to data portability
20.summary: Data subjects have the right to receive their personal data in a structured, commonly used, machine-readable format and transmit it to another controller.

22.title: Automated individual decision-making, including profiling
22.summary: Data subjects have the right not to be subject to a decision based solely on automated processing, including profiling, which produces legal effects or similarly significant effects.

25.title: Data protection by design and by default
25.summary: Controllers must implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to integrate data protection principles into processing activities and minimise data collection.

32.title: Security of processing
32.summary: Controllers and processors must implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including pseudonymisation and encryption.

35.title: Data protection impact assessment
35.summary: A DPIA is required where processing is likely to result in high risk to natural persons, particularly when using new technologies, profiling, or special category data.

46.title: Transfers subject to appropriate safeguards
46.summary: In the absence of an adequacy decision, personal data may only be transferred to third countries under SCCs, BCRs, or other approved safeguards.
